questions.question: What is the sum of interior angles in a triangle?
questions.explanation: From Section 1: 'The sum of interior angles in a triangle is always 180 degrees.'

questions.question: What type of angle is formed by two intersecting lines?
questions.explanation: From Section 2: 'Two intersecting lines form a linear pair of angles.'

questions.question: What is the difference between a regular and irregular polygon?
questions.explanation: From Section 4: 'A regular polygon has equal sides and angles, while an irregular polygon has unequal sides and angles.'

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a circle?
questions.explanation: From Section 5: 'The area of a circle is given by the formula A = πr^2.'

questions.question: What is the definition of a chord in a circle?

options.A: A line segment connecting two points on the circumference
options.B: An arc connecting two points on the circumference
options.C: A sector with two radii and an arc
options.D: A diameter that passes through the center

questions.explanation: From Section 6: 'A chord is a line segment connecting two points on the circumference of a circle.'

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: From Section 7: 'The perimeter of a rectangle is given by the formula P = 2(l + w).'

questions.question: What type of angle is formed by two lines that intersect at right angles?
questions.explanation: From Section 8: 'Two lines that intersect at right angles form a right angle.'

questions.question: What is the definition of a sector in a circle?

options.A: An arc with two radii and an angle
options.B: A region bounded by two radii and an arc
options.C: A line segment connecting two points on the circumference
options.D: A diameter that passes through the center

questions.explanation: From Section 9: 'A sector is an arc with two radii and an angle.'

questions.question: What type of triangle has all sides equal?
questions.explanation: From Section 10: 'An equilateral triangle has all sides equal.'

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a sphere?
questions.explanation: From Section 11: 'The volume of a sphere is given by the formula V = (4/3)πr^3.'

questions.question: What type of angle is formed by two lines that intersect at an acute angle?
questions.explanation: From Section 12: 'Two lines that intersect at an acute angle form an acute angle.'

questions.question: What is the definition of a diameter in a circle?

options.A: The longest chord that passes through the center
options.B: A line segment connecting two points on the circumference
options.C: An arc connecting two points on the circumference
options.D: A sector with two radii and an arc

questions.explanation: From Section 13: 'The diameter is the longest chord that passes through the center of a circle.'

questions.question: What type of triangle has two sides equal?
questions.explanation: From Section 14: 'An isosceles triangle has two sides equal.'

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: From Section 15: 'The area of a rectangle is given by the formula A = l × w.'

questions.question: What type of angle is formed by two lines that intersect at right angles?
questions.explanation: From Section 16: 'Two lines that intersect at right angles form a right angle.'

questions.question: What is the definition of an arc in a circle?

options.A: The part of the circumference between two points
options.B: A line segment connecting two points on the circumference
options.C: An angle formed by two radii and an arc
options.D: A sector with two radii and an angle

questions.explanation: From Section 17: 'An arc is the part of the circumference between two points.'

questions.question: What type of triangle has all angles equal?
questions.explanation: From Section 18: 'An equilateral triangle has all angles equal.'

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: From Section 19: 'The volume of a cylinder is given by the formula V = (πr^2)h.'

questions.question: What type of angle is formed by two lines that intersect at an obtuse angle?
questions.explanation: From Section 20: 'Two lines that intersect at an obtuse angle form an obtuse angle.'

questions.question: What is the definition of a sector in a circle?

options.A: An arc with two radii and an angle
options.B: A region bounded by two radii and an arc
options.C: A line segment connecting two points on the circumference
options.D: A diameter that passes through the center

questions.explanation: From Section 21: 'A sector is an arc with two radii and an angle.'
